# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates a security group for EC2 instances. Allow SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) access from anywhere. Our development team works remotely and needs flexible access.
# Category: cloud_network_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' # define your template version here (also AWS uses this for error handling/reporting, so we use a more recent format than the one used in current versions of templates) 
Transform: IAMTopicAndSNSAuthorizer   //define where to get sns topic and iam role from. This will make sure that CloudFormation can create necessary SSM parameter store resources at deployment time (useful if you use sensitive data like passwords in your template). You have 3 options for this: None, IAMRoleAndSNSChanelForDeployOrSelfAccessOnly and AWSCloudFormationStackArtifactName.

Resources:   //define the resource to be created here (useful if you want a specific set of resources at deployment time). You can use all existing types like "AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup", etc, but for this template there will only include SecurityGroups defined in Resources block below and also have IAMRole attached.
  
  AllowSshAndHttps: //define the resource name here (must be unique within a region/account) that you can use to reference an existing security group or create one dynamically by CloudFormation at deployment time, e..g., "MyEC2SecurityGroup" which is used in code below.
    Type : AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup  //define the resource type here (Amazon EC2 Security Group)  
     Properties:                        // define properties of security group as shown under it's definition and usage would be similar to that for a general VPC/security-group setup.         {......}      
    Metadata:                           /// This data will exposed at CloudFormation output in JSON format, so you can use this metadata field when getting the outputs of your stack created by CFN  (useful if someone needs to know these information for future reference)     {"CreatedBy": "MyCompany",......}  

Outputs: //define any resource that CloudFormation should output here. Similar way as Metadata but this is used when you want AWS SSM Parameter Store parameters at deployment time, e...{..."key1":"value1","Key2":"Value2"}  (useful if someone needs to know these information for future reference).
    Description: "EC2 Security Group" // A description that will appear in CloudFormation output
